hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns end in -a; 'ego' is a personal pronoun with irregular forms
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um; 'ego' is a personal pronoun
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have -is in the genitive; 'ego' is a personal pronoun

hints.to have: That would be the infinitive of 'habere'
hints.to go: That would be the infinitive of 'ire'
hints.to know: That would be the infinitive of 'scire'

steps.prompt: What tense and mood is the first 'sum'?

hints.present subjunctive: The present subjunctive of 'esse' would be 'sim'

hints.do, dare, dedi, datum: These are the principal parts of 'do' (to give)
hints.eo, ire, ivi, itum: These are the principal parts of 'eo' (to go)
hints.fero, ferre, tuli, latum: These are the principal parts of 'fero' (to carry)

hints.conjunction: Conjunctions like 'et' or 'sed' connect clauses differently than 'qui'
hints.interrogative adverb: An interrogative adverb would introduce a question; 'qui' here introduces a relative clause
hints.demonstrative pronoun: A demonstrative pronoun would be 'ille' or 'hic'; 'qui' refers back to the subject

steps.prompt: The infinitive of the second 'sum' translates to?

hints.to stand: That would be the infinitive of 'stare'
hints.to seem: That would be the infinitive of 'videri'
hints.to become: That would be the infinitive of 'fieri'

steps.prompt: What tense and mood is the second 'sum'?

hints.present subjunctive: The present subjunctive of 'esse' would be 'sim'

hints.possum, posse, potui, —: These are the principal parts of 'possum' (to be able)
hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the principal parts of 'sto' (to stand)
hints.fio, fieri, factus sum, —: These are the principal parts of 'fio' (to become)
